hypothesis:
## Novel Hypothesis

Implementing an adaptive hybrid batching algorithm that dynamically switches between static-64 and dynamic batching based on real-time monitoring of graph size variance within sliding windows of the training data will reduce total training time by 15-30% compared to using either algorithm alone, while maintaining equivalent model convergence properties.

**Rationale**: The paper demonstrates that static-64 performs better for longer training runs while dynamic batching is faster initially due to fewer recompilations. However, no algorithm adapts to changing graph size distributions that may occur as the dataloader progresses through shuffled training data. A hybrid approach that monitors the coefficient of variation in graph sizes over recent batches and switches algorithms when variance crosses empirically-determined thresholds could exploit the strengths of both methods throughout training.
